label: Convert "user"."kind" from native enum to text
summary: Rewrites stored "user_type" enum labels as plain text (USING "kind"::text); the value set moves to a CHECK constraint

precheck.description: ensure "kind" currently has the native enum type "user_type"
precheck.sql:
SELECT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM information_schema.columns
  WHERE table_schema = 'public'
    AND table_name = 'user'
    AND column_name = 'kind'
    AND udt_name = 'user_type'
)

execute.description: alter "kind" to text, casting stored enum labels
execute.sql: ALTER TABLE "public"."user" ALTER COLUMN "kind" TYPE text USING "kind"::text

postcheck.sql:
SELECT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM information_schema.columns
  WHERE table_schema = 'public'
    AND table_name = 'user'
    AND column_name = 'kind'
    AND udt_name = 'text'
)

precheck.sql: SELECT NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 AS "one" FROM "pg_constraint" AS "c" INNER JOIN "pg_namespace" AS "n" ON "n"."oid" = "c"."connamespace" WHERE ("c"."conname" = $1 AND "n"."nspname" = $2 AND "c"."conrelid" = to_regclass($3))) AS "result"

execute.sql: ALTER TABLE "public"."user" ADD CONSTRAINT "user_kind_check" CHECK ("kind" IN ('admin', 'user'))

postcheck.sql: SELECT EXISTS (SELECT 1 AS "one" FROM "pg_constraint" AS "c" INNER JOIN "pg_namespace" AS "n" ON "n"."oid" = "c"."connamespace" WHERE ("c"."conname" = $1 AND "n"."nspname" = $2 AND "c"."conrelid" = to_regclass($3))) AS "result"

label: Drop type user_type
summary: Drops the native enum type "user_type"; its value set is now enforced by "user_kind_check"

precheck.sql:
SELECT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM pg_type t
  JOIN pg_namespace n ON t.typnamespace = n.oid
  WHERE n.nspname = 'public'
    AND t.typname = 'user_type'
)

postcheck.sql:
SELECT NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM pg_type t
  JOIN pg_namespace n ON t.typnamespace = n.oid
  WHERE n.nspname = 'public'
    AND t.typname = 'user_type'
)
